data.description:
## Problem

In maestro session `term-1777515514586-a2eu8t` (beat maestro-ca91, lease
maestro-a85e, OpenCode iteration 3), `kno claim "maestro-ca91" --json
--lease "maestro-a85e"` exited 1 at 02:19:48.105Z with:

  error: knot 'maestro-ca91' is in state 'implementation', which is not
  a claimable queue state

But `kno show maestro-ca91`'s step_history shows an `implementation` action
step with `started_at = 02:19:48.062Z` — 42 ms BEFORE OpenCode recorded the
tool start (02:19:48.104Z). After 90 minutes of log archaeology I cannot
say from logs which of these is true:

  (a) kno claim half-transitioned: it acquired the beat, moved
      ready_for_implementation → implementation, then errored on
      validation but did NOT roll back the transition.
  (b) Some other process called kno claim first; OpenCode's call came in
      second and saw the post-transition state.
  (c) Stale read inside kno: the claimability check used cached state and
      the transition+revalidation surfaced the inconsistency.

The lease audit log, foolery server console, OpenCode permission log, and
opencode.db `part` table together show only ONE call site (OpenCode bash)
attempting kno claim during the window. So (b) is unlikely — but unlikely
is not provable.

## Target state

When this symptom recurs, the logs answer the question definitively.
Foolery captures, per dispatched agent turn:

1. Beat snapshot (state, lease_expiry_ts, last step_history entry id and
   timestamps) at:
   - lease_create_succeeded
   - lease_claim_binding_check
   - prompt_send_succeeded
   - turn_ended (BOTH on success and on non-zero exit)
2. A synthetic `kno status` poll right before the agent prompt is built,
   logged with the polled-at timestamp.
3. After turn_ended with non-zero exit, an automatic `kno show <beat>
   --json` snapshot logged in the lease audit stream, tagged with the
   session, lease, and iteration.

The snapshot pairs are written to the lease audit log with stable event
names so a future investigator can `grep` and diff them in one pass.

## Acceptance

- New lease-audit events: `beat_snapshot_pre_lease`,
  `beat_snapshot_pre_prompt`, `beat_snapshot_post_turn`. Each carries
  beat state, last step_history entry timestamps and id, and the
  active-lease state.
- New stderr banner (FOOLERY DISPATCH FORENSIC) when post-turn snapshot
  shows the beat moved into an action state but the lease has no
  matching action step or vice versa, so the inconsistency is loud the
  moment it happens (consistent with the "Fail Loudly" rule in
  CLAUDE.md).
- Repro test: a knots-stub that simulates a half-transition (action step
  created, then claim returns exit 1) and the new logging makes the
  inconsistency visible.
- The maestro-ca91 incident's missing data is enumerated in the knot
  notes so we know exactly which fields the new logging needs.

## Why this matters

User feedback: "if the logs don't tell you exactly x or y, in such a way
that you can never honestly say 'gee i dunno maybe x or maybe y', then we
need to add more logging." This knot is that addition. It does NOT try
to fix kno itself (the kno-side bug, if any, is its own knot in the kno
repo) — it makes Foolery's audit trail self-explanatory next time.
